title: Executive Order 9351—Authorizing the Secretary of the Navy to Take Possession of and Operate the Plant of the Howarth Pivoted Bearings Company at Philadelphia, Pennsylvania

flags.justification: The Secretary of the Navy is authorized to 'do all things necessary or incidental' to plant operation, with no external check on that discretion.
Possession terminates when the President determines the plant 'will be operated privately in a manner consistent with the war effort' — self-assessed, no outer time bound.
Single coherent purpose: seizure of this one plant to secure war-materiel production.
'To do all things necessary or incidental to that end' is an open-ended operational standard with no defined limits.
No incentive structure rewarding behavior contrary to the order's purpose is identifiable.
No outer time bound; termination rests entirely on presidential self-assessment.
No judicial-process-blocking or similar oversight-removal language is present.
No external party has a specified role in checking whether or when the plant should be returned to private operation.
By mid-1943 this is a routine application of the already-established plant-seizure template (see EO 8773).
The Secretary of the Navy exercises seizure authority directly; no other agency's mandate is redirected.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.
